args.name: outline
args.content:
Working outline for Mutual NDA (Acme Corp / Beta LLC, WA law, partnership evaluation)

1. Title and Parties
   - Title: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement
   - Parties: Acme Corp, a [STATE] corporation ("Acme"); Beta LLC, a [STATE] limited liability company ("Beta").
   - Effective Date: [EFFECTIVE DATE]

2. Recitals / Purpose
   - Background that parties wish to explore a potential partnership.
   - Purpose limited to evaluating a potential partnership between the parties (the "Purpose").

3. Definitions
   - "Confidential Information" (broad definition: any non-public information disclosed by either party, in any form, that is designated as confidential or should reasonably be understood as confidential given the nature and circumstances).
   - Exclusions from Confidential Information (public domain, already known, independently developed, rightfully received from third party, approved for release).
   - "Disclosing Party" and "Receiving Party" (each party may be both; mutual structure).
   - "Representatives" (employees, officers, directors, advisors, etc.).

4. Obligations of Confidentiality
   - Use restriction: use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose.
   - Non-disclosure: not disclose to third parties except to Representatives with need to know and bound by similar obligations.
   - Standard of care: at least reasonable care / same degree as own confidential info.

5. Permitted Disclosures
   - Disclosures required by law, regulation, or court order with notice and cooperation where legally permitted.

6. No License; Ownership
   - No transfer of IP rights or licenses by disclosure.
   - All Confidential Information remains property of Disclosing Party.

7. Term and Survival
   - Term of agreement: from Effective Date until [TERM END DATE OR EVENT] (e.g., [●] years after Effective Date or until either party terminates on [●] days' written notice).
   - Confidentiality obligations survive for [SURVIVAL PERIOD] after termination/expiration.

8. Return or Destruction
   - Upon request or termination, Receiving Party to return or destroy Confidential Information, subject to standard archival/backup exceptions.

9. Remedies
   - Acknowledgment that unauthorized disclosure/use may cause irreparable harm.
   - Entitlement to seek injunctive relief and other equitable remedies, in addition to any other rights and remedies at law or in equity.

10. No Obligation to Proceed
   - Clarify that neither party is obligated to proceed with any transaction or partnership.

11. No Warranty
   - Confidential Information provided "as is" without warranties.

12. Export Control / Compliance [optional]
   - [BLANK] if needed; otherwise omit or keep generic compliance statement.

13. Notices
   - Notice addresses for each party: 
     - For Acme: Acme Corp, Attn: [BLANK], Address: [BLANK], Email: [BLANK]
     - For Beta: Beta LLC, Attn: [BLANK], Address: [BLANK], Email: [BLANK]
   - Method of notice (personal delivery, courier, certified mail, email, etc.).

14. Assignment
   - No assignment without prior written consent, except to affiliates or in connection with merger/sale of substantially all assets, subject to [BLANK] conditions.

15. Governing Law and Venue
   - Governing law: laws of the State of Washington, without regard to conflict of laws principles.
   - Venue / jurisdiction: state and federal courts located in [COUNTY], Washington [BLANK if county unknown].

16. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waiver; Severability
   - Standard boilerplate: entire agreement, amendments in writing, waiver not implied, severability.

17. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures
   - May be executed in counterparts and by electronic signatures.

18. Signatures
   - Signature blocks:
     - ACME CORP
       By: ____________________________
       Name: [BLANK]
       Title: [BLANK]
       Date: [BLANK]
     - BETA LLC
       By: ____________________________
       Name: [BLANK]
       Title: [BLANK]
       Date: [BLANK]

19. Attorney-Review Disclaimer (for AI-generated draft)
   - Prominent disclaimer stating this NDA is a draft for discussion only, does not constitute legal advice, and must be reviewed, customized, and approved by a qualified attorney licensed in Washington before use.
